one_liner: LULU up 4.9% on proxy fight resolution — Chip Wilson's challenge settled with new board appointments, removing the overhang that depressed shares 8 years from highs; Wyckoff Accumulation Phase B (3/5 signals) + MACD bullish crossover + DCF $126 fair value.

price_action.vol_rank: #9 of 90 — above average but not climactic
price_action.intraday_story: LULU opened at $112, immediately pushed higher all session, touching $117.65 and closing at $117.57 — essentially at the high of day. The intraday pattern is a steady grind up with no significant pullback, suggesting buyers were in control throughout. Volume at 1.26x average is healthy for an accumulation-phase advance: sufficient to confirm institutional participation without being a climactic spike that would signal exhaustion.

news_catalyst.headline: Lululemon Resolves Chip Wilson Proxy Fight With New Board Appointments
news_catalyst.summary: The key catalyst today is the settlement of the highly-publicized Chip Wilson proxy fight — the company's founder had been publicly feuding with management and attempting to install new board members to shift strategy. The resolution (new board appointments agreed upon) removes a major governance overhang that had been suppressing institutional interest. Multiple outlets confirm the 'proxy truce reshapes board' — this is not just symbolic, it directly opens the door to institutional buyers who had been sidelined by governance uncertainty. Separately, a Barron's-style comparison piece notes LULU at an '8-year low' vs Nike at a '12-year low' for deep value investors — positioning the stock as a contrarian opportunity in premium apparel.

technical.summary:
LULU is in confirmed Wyckoff Accumulation Phase B with 3 of 5 signals — the bullish structural bias has been in place for multiple sessions. Today's 4.9% move on 1.26x volume with a close at the day's high is consistent with Phase B price action: controlled advances on moderate volume, testing resistance without climactic exhaustion. RSI at 47 is exactly mid-range with room to run to 65+ before hitting overbought.

The MACD bullish crossover is a fresh signal supporting the technical momentum. The stock is at $117.57 against the BB middle (implied around $118) and well below the BB upper at $132.68 — meaning there's room to move to resistance without the indicator signaling overbought.

For Phase B to complete and Phase C to begin: the next expected Wyckoff event is a Spring or Shakeout — a brief dip below the $110.13 support on low volume that shakes out weak hands before the markup begins. If LULU holds above $111 on any pullback and volume dries up below 3M shares, that's the entry signal. The 43% gap to resistance at $167.96 is the long-term target zone.

wyckoff_narrative:
LULU's Wyckoff structure is clean: Accumulation Phase B with 3/5 signals, bullish bias confirmed. The proxy fight resolution is exactly the type of event that Wyckoff methodology associates with smart money finishing their accumulation — governance clarity removes the 'institutional avoidance' factor that keeps a stock depressed during accumulation. Expect increased institutional buying as ESG/governance-screened funds re-enter.

The 3 detected signals suggest selling climax, automatic rally, and secondary test have all occurred — the stock has found its floor and is building a base. Phase C (Spring/Shakeout) would be the next event, potentially triggered by any broad market weakness that briefly dips LULU below $110 before reversing. Phase D markup would then target the $130-145 zone initially.

valuation.summary: DCF base case of $126 implies 7.5% upside — modest, but the negative growth rate (-6.6%) in the model reflects current revenue deceleration. If LULU can stabilize revenue at flat and return to modest positive growth, the bull case of $148 (26% upside) is more appropriate. The proxy resolution could catalyze exactly that business stabilization.

key_strengths: Premium brand pricing power with >60% gross margins
International expansion still early-stage (< 30% of revenue)
key_concerns: DCF growth rate is -6.6% — revenue deceleration needs to stabilize
fundamentals.summary: Grade B with the proxy resolution as the key fundamental catalyst. Governance clarity allows management to focus on product and international expansion rather than defending against activist pressure. The stock's 30%+ decline from highs is overdone relative to the brand's durable moat — the business hasn't deteriorated, it's decelerated.

risks: DCF growth rate of -6.6% — if revenue decline accelerates beyond current estimates, bear case of $97.51 becomes base case (17% downside)
Consumer discretionary spending compression from tariff-driven inflation could delay brand recovery
If proxy resolution doesn't stick and a second activist campaign emerges, the governance overhang returns
